gave.simpleMeaning: Put something into someone's hands or life
gave.contextMeaning: The farm provided him with room and calm
gave.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

space.simpleMeaning: An empty place where you can move
space.contextMeaning: Room and freedom to keep working
space.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

keep.contextMeaning: Continue doing it, even when life is hard
keep.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

going.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

even.simpleMeaning: Used when something surprising is true
even.contextMeaning: Shows this is true despite being far away or hard
even.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

things.simpleMeaning: Objects or parts of life around us
things.contextMeaning: The hard parts or situations he faced
things.sentence: His parents supported him, and the peaceful farm gave him the space to keep going, even when things were hard.

finally.simpleMeaning: At last, after waiting a long time
finally.contextMeaning: At last, after a long time
finally.sentence: Finally, after two years of hard work, Bell was ready to try something new.

after.contextMeaning: Later than two years of hard work
after.sentence: Finally, after two years of hard work, Bell was ready to try something new.

two.sentence: Finally, after two years of hard work, Bell was ready to try something new.

ready.sentence: Finally, after two years of hard work, Bell was ready to try something new.

try.simpleMeaning: Do something to see what happens
try.contextMeaning: Do something new to see if it works
try.sentence: Finally, after two years of hard work, Bell was ready to try something new.

made.contextMeaning: Created the first long-distance telephone call
made.sentence: In 1876, he made the world’s first long-distance telephone call.

long-distance.contextMeaning: Between places that are far apart
long-distance.sentence: In 1876, he made the world’s first long-distance telephone call.

call.contextMeaning: A phone talk made between two far-away places
call.sentence: In 1876, he made the world’s first long-distance telephone call.

spoke.simpleMeaning: Used your mouth to say words
spoke.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

traveled.simpleMeaning: Moved from one place to another
traveled.contextMeaning: Moved through the wires to another town
traveled.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

wires.simpleMeaning: Thin metal lines that carry power or messages
wires.contextMeaning: The thin lines carrying the voice message
wires.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

town.simpleMeaning: A small place where people live and work
town.contextMeaning: A small place in Ontario called Paris, near Brantford
town.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

paris.simpleMeaning: A place name for a town or city
paris.contextMeaning: A town in Ontario, not the famous French city
paris.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

kilometers.simpleMeaning: A way to measure how far something is
kilometers.contextMeaning: The place was about 13 units away in distance
kilometers.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

away.simpleMeaning: Not here; at a different place
away.contextMeaning: At a place 13 kilometers from Brantford
away.sentence: He spoke into a telephone in Brantford, and his message traveled through wires to the town of Paris, Ontario, about 13 kilometers away.

amazing.contextMeaning: A very special and exciting moment

history.simpleMeaning: Things that happened a long time ago
history.contextMeaning: The first time this had ever happened before
history.sentence: For the first time in history, a person’s voice traveled a long distance by wire.

long.simpleMeaning: Having a lot of length or time
long.contextMeaning: A very great amount in space
long.sentence: For the first time in history, a person’s voice traveled a long distance by wire.

distance.simpleMeaning: How far one place is from another
distance.contextMeaning: How far the voice traveled by wire
distance.sentence: For the first time in history, a person’s voice traveled a long distance by wire.

simple.contextMeaning: An easy, small action that changed many things
simple.sentence: This simple act changed the world.

act.contextMeaning: A small action or thing he did
act.sentence: This simple act changed the world.

because.contextMeaning: For this reason, people could talk far away
because.sentence: Because of Bell’s idea, people anywhere on Earth could talk to each other, even if they were far apart.

anywhere.contextMeaning: In every place on the planet
anywhere.sentence: Because of Bell’s idea, people anywhere on Earth could talk to each other, even if they were far apart.

earth.contextMeaning: Our planet, where all people live
earth.sentence: Because of Bell’s idea, people anywhere on Earth could talk to each other, even if they were far apart.

far.simpleMeaning: Not near; a long way away
far.contextMeaning: At a very great distance from each other
far.sentence: Because of Bell’s idea, people anywhere on Earth could talk to each other, even if they were far apart.

apart.contextMeaning: Separated from each other by a big distance
apart.sentence: Because of Bell’s idea, people anywhere on Earth could talk to each other, even if they were far apart.

smaller.contextMeaning: The world felt less far apart because talking was easier
smaller.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

brought.contextMeaning: It helped families and friends come closer in feeling
brought.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

families.contextMeaning: Groups of related people got to talk more easily
families.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

friends.simpleMeaning: People you like and spend time with
friends.contextMeaning: People who care about each other could stay in touch
friends.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

closer.contextMeaning: People felt more connected and less far from each other
closer.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

together.simpleMeaning: With other people in one place
together.contextMeaning: Families and friends felt connected as one group
together.sentence: The telephone made the world feel smaller and brought families and friends closer together.

use.contextMeaning: We talk with phones every day
use.sentence: Today, we use phones every day, but it all started with one man’s dream by the river in Brantford.

phones.simpleMeaning: Machines we use to talk to people
phones.contextMeaning: Talking devices people use every day
phones.sentence: Today, we use phones every day, but it all started with one man’s dream by the river in Brantford.

why.contextMeaning: Asking what makes this story important
why.sentence: So, why does this story matter?

matter.contextMeaning: The story is important and worth knowing
matter.sentence: So, why does this story matter?

reminds.contextMeaning: It helps us remember an important lesson
reminds.sentence: It reminds us that big ideas can begin in quiet places, and that support from family and a comfortable home can help us do great things.

begin.contextMeaning: Big ideas can start in quiet places
begin.sentence: It reminds us that big ideas can begin in quiet places, and that support from family and a comfortable home can help us do great things.

places.contextMeaning: Quiet spots where something can start
places.sentence: It reminds us that big ideas can begin in quiet places, and that support from family and a comfortable home can help us do great things.

comfortable.simpleMeaning: Nice and easy to feel good in
comfortable.contextMeaning: A home that feels safe, warm, and nice
comfortable.sentence: It reminds us that big ideas can begin in quiet places, and that support from family and a comfortable home can help us do great things.

great.sentence: It reminds us that big ideas can begin in quiet places, and that support from family and a comfortable home can help us do great things.

shows.simpleMeaning: Lets you see or understand something
shows.contextMeaning: Tells us this story teaches an important lesson
shows.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s story shows that hope, hard work, and a safe place to dream can change the world.

hope.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s story shows that hope, hard work, and a safe place to dream can change the world.

safe.contextMeaning: A place where you can feel protected
safe.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s story shows that hope, hard work, and a safe place to dream can change the world.

change.contextMeaning: Make the world become better in a new way
change.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s story shows that hope, hard work, and a safe place to dream can change the world.

sitting.simpleMeaning: Being on your bottom and not standing
sitting.contextMeaning: He was resting on a chair or place
sitting.sentence: He was sitting in his dreaming place on the farm in Brantford, looking out over the Grand River.

looking.simpleMeaning: Using your eyes to see something
looking.contextMeaning: He was watching the river with his eyes
looking.sentence: He was sitting in his dreaming place on the farm in Brantford, looking out over the Grand River.

most.contextMeaning: The one thing that matters the very best
most.sentence: The most important thing to remember from today’s story is that sometimes, a simple, quiet place—and the support of people who care about us—can help us do amazing things.

thing.contextMeaning: Something important to remember from the story
thing.sentence: The most important thing to remember from today’s story is that sometimes, a simple, quiet place—and the support of people who care about us—can help us do amazing things.

remember.contextMeaning: Not forget the important lesson from today
remember.sentence: The most important thing to remember from today’s story is that sometimes, a simple, quiet place—and the support of people who care about us—can help us do amazing things.

sometimes.contextMeaning: It happens on some days or in some cases
sometimes.sentence: The most important thing to remember from today’s story is that sometimes, a simple, quiet place—and the support of people who care about us—can help us do amazing things.

care.simpleMeaning: Feel love and want to help
care.contextMeaning: People who love us and want good things for us
care.sentence: The most important thing to remember from today’s story is that sometimes, a simple, quiet place—and the support of people who care about us—can help us do amazing things.

easier.contextMeaning: Quicker and simpler for people to do
easier.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s idea changed the world and made it easier for people everywhere to connect.

everywhere.contextMeaning: In many places around the world
everywhere.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s idea changed the world and made it easier for people everywhere to connect.

connect.contextMeaning: Talk or reach people in different places
connect.sentence: Alexander Graham Bell’s idea changed the world and made it easier for people everywhere to connect.

thanks.simpleMeaning: A nice way to say thank you
thanks.contextMeaning: A polite way to show gratitude for listening
thanks.sentence: Thanks for listening, and keep dreaming big!

listening.contextMeaning: Hearing this message until the end
listening.sentence: Thanks for listening, and keep dreaming big!

open.contextMeaning: Click to see the lesson page
open.sentence: If you want to practice more, open today's lesson page at ovienglish.com for the transcript, vocabulary support, and quiz.

lesson.simpleMeaning: A study time with a teacher
lesson.contextMeaning: Today’s study material about a topic
lesson.sentence: If you want to practice more, open today's lesson page at ovienglish.com for the transcript, vocabulary support, and quiz.

page.simpleMeaning: One screen or sheet with words
page.contextMeaning: The website screen with the lesson
page.sentence: If you want to practice more, open today's lesson page at ovienglish.com for the transcript, vocabulary support, and quiz.

quiz.contextMeaning: A small test to check learning
quiz.sentence: If you want to practice more, open today's lesson page at ovienglish.com for the transcript, vocabulary support, and quiz.

opening.contextMeaning: The start part of the school video

news.simpleMeaning: New facts or stories to hear
news.contextMeaning: A story about something from the past
news.sentence: [NEWS STORY] Let’s go back to the year 1870.

answer.contextMeaning: The sentence that tells what happened
answer.sentence: ANSWER: They moved from Scotland to Brantford, Ontario, in Canada.

moved.simpleMeaning: Went from one place to another
moved.contextMeaning: Changed homes and lived in Canada
moved.sentence: ANSWER: They moved from Scotland to Brantford, Ontario, in Canada.

decide.sentence: QUESTION: Why did Bell’s parents decide to move to Canada?

improve.sentence: ANSWER: They hoped the Canadian climate would help Alexander feel better and improve his health.

health.contextMeaning: How well Alexander’s body was feeling
health.sentence: ANSWER: They hoped the Canadian climate would help Alexander feel better and improve his health.

what.simpleMeaning: Asks for a thing or fact
what.sentence: QUESTION: What did Alexander Graham Bell do after he got healthier?

got.sentence: QUESTION: What did Alexander Graham Bell do after he got healthier?

event.contextMeaning: An important thing that happened in 1876
event.sentence: QUESTION: What important event happened in 1876 because of Bell’s idea?

between.simpleMeaning: In the middle of two things
between.contextMeaning: Used for the two places the phone call connected
between.sentence: ANSWER: Bell made the world’s first long-distance telephone call between Brantford and Paris, Ontario.

closing.contextMeaning: The last part of the talk, asking a final question
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] So, where was Alexander Graham Bell when he first thought of his big idea?
